task.en:
Using TextEdit, create a new plain-text document (if it opens in Rich Text, use Format menu → Make Plain Text first). Type these three words, one per line:
```
apple
banana
cherry
```
Then save the file as `~/Desktop/fruits.txt` (⌘S, name it `fruits.txt`, choose the Desktop, format Plain Text). Capitalization and minor autocorrect differences are fine — just get the three words on three lines in this order and save to the Desktop.

pre_command: rm -f "$HOME/Desktop/fruits.txt"; osascript -e 'tell application "TextEdit" to quit' 2>/dev/null; sleep 1
grading_command: F="$HOME/Desktop/fruits.txt"; [ -f "$F" ] && tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' < "$F" | grep -q 'apple' && tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' < "$F" | grep -q 'banana' && tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' < "$F" | grep -q 'cherry' && echo True || echo False

F="$HOME/Desktop/fruits.txt"; [ -f "$F" ] && tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' < "$F" | grep -vE '^[[:space:]]*$' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//' | awk 'NR==1{a=($0=="apple")} NR==2{b=($0=="banana")} NR==3{c=($0=="cherry")} END{ if(a&&b&&c) print "True"; else print "False" }' || echo False
